BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin in EUR after Prof. Castillo mentioned the Phase 2 irrigation expansion might raise running costs. Pre-Soo-Jin review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only, in EUR. Estate operating costs only; household budget excluded. Need to revisit with Soo-Jin once 2025 harvest actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Irrigation running cost rises by +EUR100/mo if Phase 2 service is added
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Full estate operating cost EUR 5,350 / EUR 30 contribution margin per case = 178 cases/month if covered from premium oil alone.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Combined with cooperative pooling and agritourism we stay well within buffer.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - irrigation running cost +EUR200/mo (unlikely per Castillo)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Castillo said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test ahead of the grant.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Premium-oil break-even is ~178-182 cases/month at current cost structure; current premium volume plus cooperative pooling covers operating costs with headroom. Main concern was the post-harvest dip but even at the December low we are safe.
ActionItems: Wait for Castillo to confirm the Phase 2 irrigation service quote
Revisit after year-end with Soo-Jin for proper modeling
